headlines.title: Oil prices drop 9 per cent and Wall Street rallies to a record after Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz
Stock Market Today: Market wide rally continues as Nasdaq Composite notches 13th straight gain, longest streak since 1992
VIX Crashes 44% In 3 Weeks — History Points To S&P 500 Upside
Brent Crude Tumbles Below $90 After Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
US 10-Year Treasury Yield Eases to 4.25% as Inflation Concerns Subside

notes: S&P 500 and NASDAQ both closed at new all-time highs on Friday, April 17, 2026, driven by a broad market rally.
Brent crude futures plunged over 9% after Iran announced the temporary reopening of the Strait of Hormuz amidst a 10-day ceasefire, removing a significant geopolitical risk premium.
The 10-year US Treasury yield fell by 7 basis points to 4.25%, easing inflation concerns tied to previously surging energy costs.
VIX dropped another 2.79% to 17.44, reaching its lowest levels in over two months.
